title: UCB — Immunology — briefing Q4-2025
html:
Bimzelx Quarterly Briefing -- Q4 2025 (October - December 2025)

Quarter Summary

Q4 2025 was characterized by Bimzelx's continued commercial acceleration and the completion of UCB's breakout financial year. The quarter saw Bimzelx approaching the $1B quarterly run-rate, solidifying its position as UCB's flagship product. Competitively, the IL-23 class continued to dominate overall psoriasis market share, but Bimzelx's multi-indication momentum and growing prescriber base signaled a shifting dynamic. ZoomRx's engagement with UCB entered a maintenance phase following the H1 2025 deliverables.

1. ZoomRx Activity and Account Status

Deliverable Status

No new major deliverables were produced in Q4 2025 for the HCP-Pt Conversation study, which had its final version delivered in September 2025. However, the insights continued to circulate within UCB:

  • The September 4 Final Report (with behavioral science consolidation) served as the reference document for UCB's commercial planning cycle
  • The web portal (pcr.zoomrx.com) remained active, providing UCB stakeholders with direct access to conversation recordings, transcripts, and tagged insights
  • UCB's internal teams referenced ZoomRx findings in commercial strategy discussions, particularly the segment-level analysis (Believers, Dabblers, Convert Writers)

Pipeline Opportunities

  • Wave 2 HCP-Pt Conversations: Based on the study design's flexibility, a second wave of prospective conversations was under discussion. The original proposal offered 30, 45, or 60 conversation scopes with customizable prompts
  • HS-Specific Research: With the November 2024 HS approval now nearly a year old, UCB's HS commercial team may seek dedicated HCP-Pt conversation research or promotional effectiveness tracking for the HS indication
  • PET Integration: The Bimzelx PET tracker (separate from HCP-Pt Conversations) continues across Derm and Rheumatology. Integration of HCP-Pt conversation insights with PET promotional effectiveness data remains a value-add opportunity

Account Relationships

  • Key UCB contact: JC Punzalan (UCB Immunology, primary study sponsor)
  • ZoomRx leadership: Richard Durante (Principal), Sneha Shah (Senior Manager)
  • ZoomRx delivery: Pranav UT and Vipasha Purohit (Engagement Managers)

2. Commercial and Financial Performance

UCB Full-Year 2025 Results (Reported in Early 2026)

UCB's FY 2025 financial results confirmed a transformation year driven primarily by Bimzelx:

  • UCB total revenue: EUR 7.74 billion (+26% YoY from EUR 6.15B)
  • UCB net sales: EUR 7.39 billion (+32% YoY)
  • Bimzelx net sales: EUR 2.23 billion (+267% YoY from EUR 607M)
  • Bimzelx became UCB's largest product by revenue, surpassing legacy franchises
  • Already halfway to UCB's stated target of EUR 4+ billion in peak Bimzelx sales
  • Adjusted EBITDA: EUR 2.64 billion (+79% YoY)

Growth Drivers Beyond Bimzelx

UCB's five designated growth drivers collectively delivered EUR 3.3B in 2025 revenue (>2x prior year):

  • FINTEPLA (dravet syndrome): EUR 427M (+26% YoY)
  • Rystiggo (generalized myasthenia gravis): EUR 332M (+65% YoY)
  • Bimzelx's dominance of the portfolio creates single-product concentration risk but also underscores the commercial potential

2026 Financial Guidance

UCB guided for 2026:

  • Revenue growth in high single-digit to low double-digit percentage range at constant exchange rates
  • Adjusted EBITDA growth from an underlying EUR 2.4B base at high-teens to high-20s percentage rate
  • This implies continued Bimzelx growth but at a naturally decelerating rate from the 267% 2025 growth

UCB Client Financial Signals — Q4 2025 / FY 2025

UCB total FY 2025: EUR 7.74B (+26%). Bimzelx FY: EUR 2.23B (+267%). Adjusted EBITDA: EUR 2.64B (+79%). UCB upgraded 2026 guidance to high single-digit to low double-digit revenue growth.

What UCB management said on the Q4 2025 / FY 2025 call:

  1. "Bimzelx is the fastest-growing biologic in immunology" — management positioned EUR 2.23B in effectively year 2 of US launch as category-leading velocity, setting the bar for the entire industry.
  2. BE BOLD head-to-head trial vs Skyrizi was confirmed enrolling, with management stating: "BE BOLD will be the most important data event for our franchise." PsA data expected in 2026.
  3. HS penetration was described as still building — "the HS market is underdeveloped; we're educating the market" — signaling UCB sees HS as a long-term growth lever, not an immediate revenue driver.
  4. On the biosimilar Cosentyx threat, management offered a bullish reframe: "Biosimilar IL-17A actually helps us; it proves the class and positions Bimzelx as the next-gen upgrade."

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts asked directly: "Is EUR 4B peak realistic?" UCB responded: "Our trajectory suggests EUR 4B is conservative" — the strongest peak-sales confidence signal to date.
  2. Analysts probed Briviact maturation and Fintepla trajectory — management noted: "Fintepla growth partially offsets Briviact maturation," and flagged the SKYWAY Phase 3 trial in broader epilepsy populations.
  3. On Briviact post-patent trajectory: "We're evaluating lifecycle options" — a non-committal answer that suggests Briviact defense may not be a priority.

3. Competitor Earnings Intelligence — Q4 2025

Revenue Snapshot — Q4 2025

Company Brand Q4 2025 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
AbbVie Skyrizi ~$4.3B +50% AbbVie guided Skyrizi+Rinvoq to $27B combined FY2026
AbbVie Rinvoq ~$1.1B +45% UC/CD label driving growth alongside derm/rheum
AbbVie Humira ~$1.8B Declining Transition to Skyrizi/Rinvoq "essentially complete"
J&J TREMFYA ~$1.2B +40% Hit $5.2B FY2025; UC indication approved
Novartis Cosentyx ~$1.4B +4% Managing toward biosimilar entry; IV SC conversion ongoing
Eli Lilly Taltz ~$750M -2% Flat; Lilly commercial attention almost entirely on GLP-1
Amgen Otezla ~$560M -7% Declining; ICOTYDE (J&J oral IL-23) expected to accelerate erosion
Bimzelx ~EUR 600M est. (Q4) ~+200% Approaching EUR 2.23B FY2025; on track for peak sales

AbbVie — Skyrizi ($4.3B) + Rinvoq ($1.1B) + Humira ($1.8B)

From AbbVie Q4 2025 / FY 2025 earnings commentary:

  1. Skyrizi ~$4.3B, +50% — $27B combined FY2026 guidance: AbbVie guided Skyrizi+Rinvoq to $27B combined for FY2026. This projection means the two-product immunology franchise alone would be nearly the size of J&J's entire Innovative Medicine division. For UCB, this signals that AbbVie's share of voice and promotional intensity in immunology will only increase.

  2. AbbVie claiming 75% IL-23 new patient starts in IBD: Management stated Skyrizi captures ~75% of IL-23 new starts in frontline IBD. While Bimzelx doesn't compete in IBD (IL-17 inhibitors lack GI indications), this claim reinforces IL-23 as the "preferred biologic class" narrative — spillover positioning that disadvantages all IL-17 agents in dermatologist conversations.

  3. Humira transition "essentially complete": AbbVie described the Humira-to-Skyrizi/Rinvoq switch as finished in derm and rheum. Full promotional resources now target Skyrizi, meaning Bimzelx faces maximum competitive intensity.

Implication for UCB: AbbVie's ~$7.2B quarterly immunology franchise is the competitive context. Bimzelx cannot win on breadth — it must win on depth: superior PsA efficacy (BE BOLD trial upcoming), faster skin clearance (PASI 100 rates), and HS where AbbVie has no IL-23 competitor.

Johnson & Johnson — TREMFYA (~$1.2B, +40%)

From J&J Q4 2025 / FY 2025 earnings commentary:

  1. TREMFYA hit $5.2B FY2025; UC indication approved: UC approval gives TREMFYA cross-indication momentum similar to Skyrizi. The GI expansion further entrenches IL-23 in treatment algorithms.

  2. ICOTYDE (oral IL-23) launching for mild-moderate psoriasis: J&J's oral IL-23 receptor antagonist targets the mild-moderate segment where Otezla has been dominant. If ICOTYDE succeeds, it creates another IL-23 touchpoint in derm practices — further normalizing IL-23 as the default class.

Implication for UCB: TREMFYA's UC approval and ICOTYDE's entry mean IL-23 now spans oral-to-injectable and derm-to-GI. Bimzelx's competitive moat must be built on specific clinical superiority claims (PsA, HS) rather than class-level positioning.

Novartis — Cosentyx (~$1.4B, +4%)

From Novartis Q4 2025 earnings commentary:

  1. Managing toward biosimilar entry: Growth essentially flat at +4%. Novartis is running IV SC conversion to extend Cosentyx's lifecycle. HS approval providing a modest boost.

  2. Novartis pivoting investment: Attention shifting to Kisqali (oncology) and rare disease. Cosentyx is entering the "harvest phase."

Implication for UCB: Cosentyx's deceleration into biosimilar territory creates a "graduation" opportunity for Bimzelx — patients on first-gen IL-17 whose insurance covers Cosentyx biosimilar can be positioned for "upgrade to next-gen IL-17A/F" when they need better outcomes.

Eli Lilly — Taltz (~$750M, -2%)

From Lilly Q4 2025 context:

  1. Taltz flat, commercially deprioritized: Lilly's promotional energy is almost entirely on GLP-1. Taltz is self-maintaining with no incremental investment.

Implication for UCB: Taltz patients losing rep support and commercial attention represent low-hanging-fruit switchers. Bimzelx's mechanism story (dual IL-17A/F vs. single IL-17A) gives a clear clinical rationale for the upgrade.

Amgen — Otezla (~$560M, -7%)

From Amgen Q4 2025 context:

  1. Declining accelerating at -7%: ICOTYDE's anticipated entry is expected to further erode Otezla's oral convenience moat by offering IL-23-class efficacy in oral form.

Implication for UCB: Otezla's structural decline validates that efficacy beats convenience in dermatology. This supports Bimzelx's positioning — injectable but superior — against the narrative that oral options will dominate.

Synthesis: What Q4 Competitor Earnings Tell UCB

  1. AbbVie's FY2026 guidance of $27B for Skyrizi+Rinvoq sets the competitive ceiling — Bimzelx at ~EUR 2.23B FY2025 is roughly one-eighth of AbbVie's immunology franchise. The game is niche dominance, not volume competition.

  2. IL-23 is now a cross-specialty, multi-route class — with Skyrizi in GI, TREMFYA pursuing UC, and ICOTYDE launching oral — IL-23 is everywhere. Bimzelx's differentiation must be CLINICAL (BE BOLD head-to-head), not class-based.

  3. Cosentyx and Taltz combined ~$2.15B quarterly are in managed decline — this is a massive addressable patient pool for Bimzelx. Every patient stepping off first-gen IL-17 or deprioritized Taltz is a conversion target.

  4. BE BOLD trial (bimekizumab vs. risankizumab in PsA) results in early 2026 will be the most important data event — if positive, it gives Bimzelx the first head-to-head superiority claim against an IL-23, fundamentally changing the competitive narrative.

4. Competitive Landscape Updates

Skyrizi Continues to Dominate PsO

  • Skyrizi maintained its ~45% US biologic prescription share in psoriasis through year-end 2025
  • AbbVie boosted its 2025 revenue forecast by $400M mid-year, largely on Skyrizi and Rinvoq performance
  • Skyrizi's Crohn's disease and UC approvals continued to create cross-indication prescribing momentum in GI/derm offices

Tremfya Expansion

  • Tremfya completed 2025 at $5.2B in global sales, up 40% from 2024
  • J&J continued pursuit of UC indication for Tremfya, which would further broaden its competitive footprint
  • Tremfya remained the second IL-23 choice for dermatologists, often positioned for patients prioritizing less frequent dosing and "clean" safety profile

IL-17 Class Economics Shifting

  • Biosimilar secukinumab (Cosentyx) competition moved closer to reality, with multiple candidates advancing
  • If biosimilar Cosentyx enters the market at significant discount, it could either cannibalize Bimzelx (by making the IL-17 class cheaper via an older product) or accelerate IL-17 adoption overall (by increasing class familiarity and reducing access barriers)
  • Taltz maintained its niche among HCPs comfortable with IL-17A but unfamiliar with Bimzelx

Safety Data Evolution

  • Real-world pharmacovigilance data for Bimzelx continued to accumulate through 2025, with no new major safety signals emerging
  • The IL-23 vs. IL-17 safety comparison became more nuanced: a multicenter real-world study (presented AAD March 2026) found IL-23 inhibitors reduce mortality and infection risks but increase NMSC and malignancy risks relative to IL-17 agents
  • This emerging data could potentially rebalance the safety narrative in favor of IL-17s, though it is too early for clinical practice impact

5. Regulatory and Clinical Milestones

Indication Timeline Review

By end of Q4 2025, Bimzelx had a complete US indication portfolio:

  • PsO (October 2023)
  • PsA, nr-axSpA, AS (September 2024)
  • HS (November 2024)

No additional US regulatory filings were publicly announced during Q4 2025, though UCB's pipeline included ongoing clinical development that could yield future indication expansions.

BE BOLD Trial Status

The pivotal BE BOLD head-to-head trial (bimekizumab vs. risankizumab in PsA) was ongoing during Q4 2025, with topline results expected in early 2026. This trial's outcome was anticipated to be a major catalyst for Bimzelx's competitive positioning in PsA and, by extension, in the broader IL-17 vs. IL-23 narrative. Results were ultimately announced in March 2026 (see Q1 2026 briefing).

6. What ZoomRx Should Know for Planning

Account Growth Potential

UCB's Bimzelx momentum creates a favorable environment for expanding ZoomRx's research scope:

  • HS module: The most immediate new business opportunity. UCB's HS commercial team will need market insights as the indication moves past its first anniversary
  • Rheumatology-specific research: With BE BOLD data imminent, UCB will need research support to understand how rheumatologists respond to head-to-head PsA data
  • Longitudinal tracking: Repeating HCP-Pt Conversations in 2026 with the same panel or comparable HCPs would allow measurement of prescribing behavior change over time -- a high-value deliverable

Competitive Intelligence Value

ZoomRx's position running research for both UCB (Bimzelx) and J&J (Tremfya/other programs) and other clients in the immunology space provides a unique vantage point. While all research is client-confidential, ZoomRx's general TA knowledge of the IL-17/IL-23 competitive dynamic informs better study design and more insightful analysis for all clients.

Key Questions Entering Q1 2026

  1. Will BE BOLD head-to-head data change HCP behavior in PsA prescribing?
  2. Is Bimzelx's HS uptake tracking ahead of or behind expectations?
  3. How are Bimzelx Dabblers evolving -- are they becoming Believers or reverting to competitors?
  4. Has access parity improved, or is insurance friction still a primary barrier?
  5. What impact does the emerging IL-17 safety favorability data (vs. IL-23 on NMSC) have on prescriber perception?

AbbVie boosted its 2025 revenue forecast by $400M mid-year, largely on Skyrizi and Rinvoq performance

This emerging data could potentially rebalance the safety narrative in favor of IL-17s, though it is too early for clinical practice impact

PsA, nr-axSpA, AS (September 2024)
HS (November 2024)

No additional US regulatory filings were publicly announced during Q4 2025, though UCB's pipeline included ongoing clinical development that could yield future indication expansions.
